cot_prefix: Okay, let me read through the text carefully. The story is about a mother who wanted a flower garden but was too busy raising her five children—Matthew, Marvin, the twins Alisa and Alan, and Helen. She mentions that money and time were tight. Often when the kids asked for something expensive, she had to tell them that money doesn't grow on trees. Later, after her children are all grown up and gone, on Mother's Day, she discovers a "money tree" in her yard with $100 taped to it. Marvin had planted it and promised to dig up a garden bed. The other children gave her gardening tools and items. Now, her garden is beautiful, and she feels her children are with her when she's tending to it. She ends by saying she's not sure if money grows on trees, but she knows love does. So, the question is asking what we know about the mother. Looking at the options: (A) She is a bad-tempered woman. The text doesn't indicate she's bad-tempered. She's just practical and trying to manage with what she has. So probably not A. (B) She believes money can grow on a tree now. The last line says she's still not sure that money grows on trees, but she knows love does. So B is incorrect. (C) She is a writer. There's no mention of her being a writer. The story is about her garden and children. So C is unlikely. (D) She is hard-working in housekeeping. She had five children, was busy raising them, worked hard, and eventually created a
